experiment: Lever-2 multi-hop constraint survival (live validation of the transitive-injection fix)
substrate: Fork-A ledger contract; same model (sonnet x3)
topology: NATURAL chain architect -> to_wire -> from_wire, where from_wire directly needs ONLY to_wire (not architect). The hop-1 constraint must reach hop-3 transitively.
fix_under_test: coordinate._transitive_ancestors + _build_command ancestors closure (commit 2691520): the residue is injected from the full needs-closure, not just direct needs.
verdict: H2 CONFIRMED (N=3, live): the transitive-injection fix carries a hop-1 constraint to hop-3 on a natural chain where hop-3 only directly needs hop-2. Before the fix, direct-needs-only injection would lose it at hop-3 (proven by unit test test_derived_survives_multiple_hops). The clean 8/8 single-effective-hop confirm (Exp. 4) now extends to a real 3-hop chain.
note: Pre-fix, the Exp. 4 task made from_wire explicitly need architect so the constraint survived; this run uses the natural topology (needs:[to_wire] only) and relies on the fix.
